title: Open Bugs
main_idea: Bugs to loop through and fix one at a time. we only need to consume these on at a time.
reason: Can't have bugs! Tracking here makes it easy to go through them as they come up.

requirements.name: No Window Layout Persistence
requirements.description: Should persist terminal setup through app launches within a session.
requirements.ex1: if split to three, should open it that way
requirements.ex2: if split into 2, open that way
requirements.ex3: if split into more than 1, and sizing is adjusted through drag handles, open it that way.

requirements.name: Broken Formatting When Switching Sessions
requirements.issue: When we switch back to a session, the terminal is formatted poorly, but resizing reset it to what it should be.  we need to fix that.  it currently seems to make the content real narrow until resizing resets something
requirements.context1: when switching to a session with terminals that only have 0 no AI output, it seems to be fine
requirements.context2: When switching to a window with only 1 terminal, and AI output, it shows the malformation for a literal second or two, then something reset it correctly
requirements.context3: I really in fact only seems to be a window with AI output. but fixes on resizing

requirements: No keyboard shortcut to close active terminal, reducing the open windows and handling that accordingly
ctrl + shift + backslash is not opening the second window, but it does switch focus between open panels when they're there
deleting one panel, the panel it shifted to on close broke.  it was split three way, and the two secondary terminals dissapeared, thought ctrl shift = opened them back up, in the same state.
the shortcut to open settings doens't close it too.  i have a feeling any menu we have other than the keyboard shortcuts behaves the same way.  let's fix that
the separator between the two secondary panels dissapeared. ts really confusing it looks like one big terminal when that happens
the whole app seems to be too tall.  you can scroll it small bit.
we need another small indicator of focused terminal, like changing border color or something
clicking on a terminal should focus it.  it doesn't right now, and that's a step down from normal terminals, or vscode experience, which we're modeling after

when a terminal has a long history, switching back to that session the terminal seems to scroll really fast, maybe from all the way top, to bottom before settling back to normal.  why isn't the terminal just doing what it does, just like if I were using vscode? is it because we remove it from the screen? would this all work better if we just hid it with css instead of removing from screen entirely?
i can't copy from the terminal.  can't right click, and ctrl shift c doesn't work either.
if any tests are creating test sessions, we need them to be cleaned up.  and we need a rule that any ai running test sessions to verify things need to clean them up
